44	You audit the Lean **as Lean**. Your directive will NOT include the project strategy, the blueprint, or "what we are trying to prove" — and that is intentional. The point is to detect mathematically-wrong definitions and dead-end proofs *without* being swayed by what the project assumes should be true. A definition is wrong if it is wrong by reading the Lean alone (e.g. its body contradicts standard mathematical usage of the named concept); you do not need to know the strategy to spot that.

49	- `-- This proof is incomplete but works for now.`
50	- `-- TODO: replace before merging.`
51	- `-- Stand-in definition until we figure out the right one.`

Both `kimi` and `kimi-anthropic` use the same `MOONSHOT_API_KEY`; the latter speaks Moonshot's Anthropic-compatible Messages endpoint and supports `--think`. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup (cross-check a claim against Hartshorne, fetch an arXiv paper, summarize a Stacks tag) consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.

When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \

42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Treat each dispatch as blocking — wait for its report before acting on it.** Don't *deliberately* set `run_in_background: true`. But note the wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and only returns once the child finishes and its report is on disk), so the harness may *auto-background* a long-running dispatch and hand you a task ID immediately — that's expected and fine. When it does, await that task / poll for the report file at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` (the wrapper's one-line status and exit 0 confirm completion) before you read or act on the result. The thing to avoid is firing a dispatch and then continuing to plan as if it had already returned; whether the call blocked inline or was auto-backgrounded, the rule is the same: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.

Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. 10	# Čech computation of the higher direct images `Rⁱ f_*` (unconditional)
11	
12	This file constructs the higher derived direct images `Rⁱ f_* F` for `i ≥ 1`
13	**without appealing to injective resolutions** in the category of sheaves of
14	modules. The companion `Cohomology/HigherDirectImage.lean` defines `Rⁱ f_*` as a
15	right derived functor, which requires the ambient category of `O_X`-modules to
16	have enough injectives — a property not currently available for sheaves of
17	modules over a sheaf of rings whose value category varies over the site. The
18	Čech approach developed here sidesteps the issue: it computes `Rⁱ f_* F` as the
19	cohomology of an explicit complex built from the pushforwards of `F` over the
20	finite intersections of an affine open cover, producing an **unconditional**
21	construction of `Rⁱ f_*` for quasi-coherent `F` and separated quasi-compact `f`.
22	
23	Throughout, `f : X ⟶ S` is a quasi-compact, separated morphism of schemes (so
24	all finite intersections of an affine open cover of `X` are again affine), and
25	`F : X.Modules` is a quasi-coherent `O_X`-module. A base change of `f` along
26	`g : S' ⟶ S` is recorded by a cartesian square
27	```
28	  X' --g'--> X
29	  |f'        |f
30	  v          v
31	  S' --g---> S
32	```
33	with `F' = (g')^* F` the pullback of `F` to `X'`.
34	
35	The six main declarations are:
36	
37	* `AlgebraicGeometry.CechNerve` — the (augmented) Čech nerve of an affine open
38	  cover, an augmented cosimplicial object of `O_X`-modules.
39	* `AlgebraicGeometry.CechComplex` — the relative Čech complex in `QCoh(S)`, a
40	  cochain complex of `O_S`-modules whose degree-`p` term is the product of the
41	  pushforwards of `F` over the `(p+1)`-fold intersections of the cover.
42	* `AlgebraicGeometry.CechAcyclic.affine` — Čech acyclicity on affines: the Čech
43	  complex of a standard cover of an affine scheme has vanishing cohomology in
44	  all positive degrees (Serre vanishing for quasi-coherent sheaves on affines).
45	* `AlgebraicGeometry.cech_computes_higherDirectImage` — the cohomology of the
46	  relative Čech complex is canonically isomorphic to `Rⁱ f_* F` wherever the
47	  derived functor is defined.
48	* `AlgebraicGeometry.cechHigherDirectImage` — the **unconditional** `i`-th higher
49	  direct image, defined as the `i`-th cohomology sheaf of the relative Čech
50	  complex (no enough-injectives hypothesis required).
51	* `AlgebraicGeometry.cech_flatBaseChange` — flat base change for the
52	  unconditional Čech higher direct images.
53	
54	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_CechHigherDirectImage.tex`.

156	/-- **The substrate operation `⊗` on `Scheme.Modules X`.**

158	For a scheme `X` and `M, N : X.Modules`, the tensor product

161	`M` and `N` (affine-locally `(M ⊗_X N)(Spec A) = M(Spec A) ⊗_A N(Spec A)`).

164	`PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` through the sheafification functor on
165	the small Zariski site of `X` (fully defined, no `sorry`). -/
166	noncomputable def tensorObj {X : Scheme.{u}} (M N : X.Modules) : X.Modules :=
167	  ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj

169	    SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf)

101	The genuine construction of the {\v C}ech nerve `CechNerve` factors through two
102	ingredients that are independent of one another:
103	
104	* a *geometric* backbone — the augmented {\v C}ech nerve of the cover, an
105	  augmented simplicial **scheme** over `X` (the iterated fibre powers of
106	  `∐ᵢ Uᵢ` over `X`), which exists unconditionally because `Scheme` has all
107	  finite limits; and
108	* a *push-pull* functor `(Over X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ X.Modules`, `(Y, p) ↦ p_* p^* F`, that
109	  turns the simplicial scheme over `X` into the cosimplicial `O_X`-module
110	  `CechNerve`.

112	The backbone (`coverArrow`, `coverCechNerve`) is built here axiom-clean. The
113	push-pull functor is the remaining gap: its `map_comp` requires the
114	`pushforwardComp` / `pullbackComp` coherence isomorphisms (the same coherence
115	quagmire active in `Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean`), so `CechNerve` itself is
116	left as the single genuine hole.

118	Independently of the nerve, the passage *from* an augmented cosimplicial
119	`O_X`-module *to* the relative {\v C}ech cochain complex in `QCoh(S)` is pure,
120	coherence-free plumbing (`relativeCechComplexOfNerve`): forget the augmentation,
121	push forward along `f` via `CosimplicialObject.whiskering`, and take the
122	alternating coface-map cochain complex. We record it here so that `CechComplex`
123	is *defined* in terms of `CechNerve` — closing `CechNerve` axiom-clean
124	immediately yields an axiom-clean `CechComplex`. -/

154	/-- The canonical isomorphism `free PUnit ≅ unit R`: the free module on a single
155	generator is the unit module. -/
156	noncomputable def freeUnitIso :
157	    SheafOfModules.free.{u} (R := R) PUnit.{u + 1} ≅ SheafOfModules.unit R :=
158	  Limits.coproductUniqueIso (fun (_ : PUnit.{u + 1}) ↦ SheafOfModules.unit R)
159	
160	/-- The canonical generating sections of `unit R`: one generator (the image of
161	the free generator under `freeUnitIso`). -/
162	noncomputable def unitGenerators : (SheafOfModules.unit R).GeneratingSections where
163	  I := PUnit.{u + 1}
164	  s := (SheafOfModules.unit R).freeHomEquiv freeUnitIso.hom
165	  epi := by
166	    rw [Equiv.symm_apply_apply]
167	    infer_instance

169	/-- The canonical finite presentation of the unit module `unit R`: one generator,
170	no relations (its generating map is an isomorphism, so the relation kernel is the
171	zero object). -/
172	noncomputable def unitPresentation : (SheafOfModules.unit R).Presentation where
173	  generators := unitGenerators
174	  relations :=
175	    { I := PEmpty.{u + 1}
176	      s := PEmpty.elim
177	      epi := by
178	        have hiso : IsIso (unitGenerators (R := R)).π := by
179	          dsimp only [unitGenerators, SheafOfModules.GeneratingSections.π]
180	          rw [Equiv.symm_apply_apply]
181	          infer_instance
182	        have : Mono (unitGenerators (R := R)).π := inferInstance
183	        have hz : Limits.IsZero (Limits.kernel (unitGenerators (R := R)).π) :=
184	          Limits.isZero_kernel_of_mono _
185	        exact ⟨fun g h _ => hz.eq_of_src g h⟩ }

188	  isFiniteType_generators := ⟨inferInstanceAs (Finite PUnit.{u + 1})⟩
189	  isFiniteType_relations := ⟨inferInstanceAs (Finite PEmpty.{u + 1})⟩

194	
195	/-- **The over↔restrict trivialisation bridge.** Transports the scheme-level
196	trivialisation `e : M.restrict U.ι ≅ 𝒪_U` (an isomorphism in
197	`SheafOfModules (↑U).ringCatSheaf`, the category of modules on the *open
198	subscheme* `U`) to a slice-level trivialisation
199	`M.over U ≅ 𝒪_U` (in `SheafOfModules (X.ringCatSheaf.over U)`, the category of
200	modules on the *slice site* `J.over U`).

203	`U.ι.opensFunctor : Opens ↥U ≌ {V ∈ Opens X | V ≤ U} = Over U` induces an
204	equivalence `SheafOfModules (↑U).ringCatSheaf ≌ SheafOfModules (X.ringCatSheaf.over U)`
205	identifying `M.restrict U.ι ↦ M.over U` and `unit ↦ unit`. That modules-level

217	noncomputable def chartOverIso (M : X.Modules) (U : X.Opens)
218	    (e : M.restrict U.ι ≅ SheafOfModules.unit (U : Scheme).ringCatSheaf) :

220	  Scheme.Modules.chartOverIso U M e

223	presentation"). On a trivialising chart `U` — i.e. with an isomorphism
224	`M|_U ≅ 𝒪_U` — the restriction `M.over U` admits the trivial finite free
225	presentation (one generator, no relations), obtained by transporting the
226	canonical presentation of `SheafOfModules.unit` along the trivialisation
227	(through the `chartOverIso` bridge). -/
228	noncomputable def IsLocallyTrivial.chartPresentation (M : X.Modules) (U : X.Opens)
229	    (e : M.restrict U.ι ≅ SheafOfModules.unit (U : Scheme).ringCatSheaf) :
230	    (M.over U).Presentation := by
231	  exact SheafOfModules.Presentation.ofIsIso (R := X.ringCatSheaf.over U)
232	    (M := SheafOfModules.unit (X.ringCatSheaf.over U)) (N := SheafOfModules.over M U)
233	    (chartOverIso M U e).inv (unitPresentation (R := X.ringCatSheaf.over U))

236	    (e : M.restrict U.ι ≅ SheafOfModules.unit (U : Scheme).ringCatSheaf) :
237	    (IsLocallyTrivial.chartPresentation M U e).IsFinite := by
238	  dsimp only [IsLocallyTrivial.chartPresentation]

244	A locally trivial module on a scheme is finitely presented: assemble a
245	`QuasicoherentData` from the trivialising affine cover
246	(`exists_trivializing_cover`), whose presentation at each index is the finite
247	free presentation of `chartPresentation`, and feed it to
248	`SheafOfModules.IsFinitePresentation.mk`. No stalk computation or
249	neighbourhood-spreading is involved. -/
250	theorem IsLocallyTrivial.isFinitePresentation {M : X.Modules}
251	    (hM : IsLocallyTrivial M) :
252	    M.IsFinitePresentation := by
253	  obtain ⟨I, U, hUaff, hUtop, hUiso⟩ := hM.exists_trivializing_cover
254	  -- assemble the quasi-coherent data, one finite chart presentation per index;
255	  -- `shrink` lands the index type in the site-object universe demanded by
256	  -- `IsFinitePresentation`'s existential.
257	  let q : M.QuasicoherentData :=
258	    { I := I
259	      X := U
260	      coversTop := by
261	        -- the trivialising affine opens cover the terminal object of the site
262	        intro W x hxW
263	        have hx : x ∈ iSup U := by rw [hUtop]; trivial
264	        rw [TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_iSup] at hx
265	        obtain ⟨i, hi⟩ := hx
266	        exact ⟨U i ⊓ W, homOfLE inf_le_right, ⟨i, ⟨homOfLE inf_le_left⟩⟩, hi, hxW⟩
267	      presentation := fun i => IsLocallyTrivial.chartPresentation M (U i) (hUiso i).some }
268	  have hsh : q.shrink.IsFinitePresentation := by
269	    apply SheafOfModules.QuasicoherentData.IsFinitePresentation.mk
270	    intro i
271	    exact inferInstanceAs (IsLocallyTrivial.chartPresentation M _ _).IsFinite
272	  exact { exists_quasicoherentData := ⟨q.shrink, hsh⟩ }

275	`isFinitePresentation`). Finite presentation implies finite type by Mathlib's
276	`SheafOfModules.instIsFiniteTypeOfIsFinitePresentation` and quasi-coherence by
277	`SheafOfModules.instIsQuasicoherentOfIsFinitePresentation`. -/
278	theorem IsLocallyTrivial.isFiniteType {M : X.Modules}
279	    (hM : IsLocallyTrivial M) :
280	    M.IsFiniteType := by
281	  haveI := hM.isFinitePresentation
282	  infer_instance
